Title: Soybean Auxin Transporter PIN3 Regulates Nitrate Acquisition to Improve Nitrogen Use and Seed Traits

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout in soybean; promoter:NLS-Venus reporter; Xenopus oocyte 15NO3- uptake assay; RT-qPCR; genetic interaction with pin3ab
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disruption partially suppresses elevated nitrogen accumulation and pavement-cell phenotypes in pin3ab; no broader agronomic tradeoff directly reported for npf2.13 alone
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports plant nitrogen nutrition, photosynthetic capacity, growth, and contributes to seed oil accumulation indirectly through improved nitrogen use
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a key functional nitrate transporter transcriptionally activated by ARF10 and STF3; central downstream effector of the PIN3-dependent regulatory module
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Mediates cellular nitrate transport across the plasma membrane rather than a chemical conversion
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for nitrate uptake in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance nitrate acquisition and nitrogen-use efficiency, likely best combined with upstream regulatory manipulation; caution that excessive nitrogen uptake may affect developmental balance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NPF2.13 transcript is strongly up-regulated in pin3 higher-order mutants. 2. NPF2.13 promoter activity is higher in pin3abdCR2 than WT. 3. GmNPF2.13 transports nitrate in Xenopus oocytes at levels comparable to AtNPF6.3. 4. Knockout of NPF2.13 compromises the elevated nitrogen accumulation and partially restores leaf-cell defects of pin3ab, showing it is required for the enhanced nitrate uptake phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes nitrate uptake and nitrogen accumulation; required for the enhanced nitrogen acquisition phenotype of pin3ab mutants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Promoter activity detected in leaves; stronger in pin3abdCR2 than WT
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and other sink organs including seed coat and seeds (as nitrogen accumulation sites)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf tissue; promoter activity elevated in pin3abdCR2 under low- and high-nitrate comparisons in study context

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GH3 acyl acid amido synthetase family
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in pin3ab mutant background; phenotype rescue; nitrogen-content measurement
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing auxin levels may counter desirable nitrate-acquisition gains
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls plant growth and development; in this study links auxin homeostasis to nitrate uptake regulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used functionally to test whether auxin overaccumulation causes pin3ab phenotypes; not the main causal nitrate transporter but directly relevant to nutrient phenotype via auxin conjugation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Conjugates IAA to amino acids, producing inactive IAA-amino acid forms
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Overexpression construct promoter not clearly specified in text
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful mechanistic tool to tune auxin homeostasis, but likely counterproductive if the goal is to maximize nitrate uptake driven by PIN3 disruption
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH3.1 overexpression in pin3ab reduced curly-leaf and pavement-cell defects. 2. GH3.1 overexpression partially restored the elevated leaf nitrogen content of pin3ab toward WT. 3. These results support that auxin overaccumulation mediates the nitrate-acquisition phenotype of pin3 mutants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces free auxin and partially suppresses the elevated nitrogen accumulation phenotype of pin3ab

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Auxin efflux transporter controlling hormone distribution
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PIN-FORMED auxin efflux carrier with transmembrane domains and central hydrophilic loop
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR mutants; PIN3apro:PIN3a-GFP complementation/localization; NMT IAA efflux assay; 15NO3- uptake assay; RNA-seq; pharmacology (NPA, BFA, CHX, MG132); ubiquitination immunoblot
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes curly leaves, altered pavement-cell polarity, reduced nodulation/nitrogen fixation, and slight yield decrease under nitrate starvation in one field test, though improves seed oil and usually not yield per area under fertilized conditions
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Auxin transport upstream of nitrate uptake regulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: High environmental nitrate promotes PIN3a ubiquitination, degradation, and enrichment at cell-wall junctions
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transport-mediated negative regulation of auxin accumulation and downstream transcriptional activation; protein abundance controlled by ubiquitin/proteasome-mediated degradation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a negative regulator of nitrate acquisition; disruption increases leaf and sink-organ nitrogen accumulation and raises seed oil content
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PIN3a is highly expressed in mature soybean leaves and localizes to the plasma membrane/cell junctions. 2. High nitrate accelerates PIN3a ubiquitination and degradation, enriching remaining protein at cell-wall junctions. 3. PIN3a deficiency reduces IAA efflux, causes auxin overaccumulation, and up-regulates nitrate transport genes. 4. pin3ab mutants show enhanced nitrate uptake, elevated nitrogen accumulation, and increased seed oil in the field.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negatively affects NPF2.13 pathway indirectly by preventing auxin overaccumulation and downstream ARF10/STF3/4 activation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nitrogen improves growth and photosynthesis; increased seed oil enhances nutritional and agronomic value
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strong in mature leaves, especially pavement cells and vasculature; promoter:GUS active in mature leaves
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High nitrate (5.3 mM KNO3); low nitrate; rhizobia co-supply; NPA, BFA, CHX, MG132 treatments for mechanistic assays
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major upstream brake on nitrate uptake and seed-oil improvement through auxin transport control
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No specific complex experimentally validated; nitrate-induced ubiquitination machinery remains unknown
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for CRISPR editing to improve nitrogen-use efficiency and seed oil in elite soybean backgrounds, with monitoring of nodulation and possible stress/yield tradeoffs
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane; enriched at tripartite cell-wall junctions under high nitrate
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in mature leaves; protein relocalization and degradation enhanced by high nitrate

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Auxin efflux transporter controlling hormone distribution
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR single/double/triple mutants; expression analysis; IAA efflux and 15NO3- uptake phenotyping in pin3ab/pin3abd lines
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Contributes to altered leaf morphology and reduced nodulation/nitrogen fixation when disrupted with PIN3a
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Auxin transport upstream of nitrate uptake regulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Nitrate-responsive context inferred; direct degradation dynamics characterized mainly for PIN3a
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transport-mediated negative regulation of auxin accumulation and downstream nitrate-uptake signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with PIN3a negatively regulates nitrate acquisition; loss contributes to increased nitrogen accumulation and seed oil
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PIN3b is highly expressed in mature leaves together with PIN3a. 2. Single mutants show limited phenotype, whereas double/triple mutants reveal strong effects on auxin transport and nitrate acquisition. 3. PIN3b acts redundantly with PIN3a as a negative regulator of nitrate uptake and downstream seed/oil traits.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirect negative effect on NPF2.13 pathway via auxin transport
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nitrogen supports growth/photosynthesis; seed oil has nutritional value
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Abundant in mature leaves; strong promoter:GUS signal in mature leaves
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High nitrate; low nitrate; rhizobia co-supply
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Core redundant upstream controller with PIN3a
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Best manipulated jointly with PIN3a for trait improvement
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane (inferred from conserved PIN3 ortholog behavior)

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual-luciferase assays; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; promoter:GUS; co-expression analysis; auxin-related phenotyping in pin3 mutants
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Activation may amplify nitrate uptake and developmental changes linked to auxin overaccumulation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of nitrate uptake and light-signaling modules downstream of auxin
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Auxin overaccumulation caused by PIN3 deficiency; exogenous auxin context supports auxin-responsive network
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes nitrate uptake by directly activating NPF2.13 and STF3 in pin3 mutant context
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ARF transcripts are up-regulated in pin3 mutants, and ARF10 is expressed in leaf pavement cells. 2. ARF10 directly binds AGACA/TGTCT-related motifs in the NPF2.13 promoter and activates its transcription. 3. ARF10 also binds and activates the STF3 promoter. 4. ARF10 is a central auxin-responsive activator linking PIN3 deficiency to enhanced nitrate uptake.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improved nitrogen acquisition supports growth, photosynthesis, and seed quality traits
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in leaf pavement cells; transcripts up-regulated in pin3 mutants
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Auxin overaccumulation in pin3 mutants; exogenous NAA is consistent with activation of related pathway
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential auxin-responsive activator connecting impaired auxin efflux to nitrate transporter induction
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator to increase nitrogen-use efficiency, but deployment should consider auxin-related morphology effects
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf pavement cells; elevated transcript abundance in pin3ab and pin3abdCR2

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TGACG-motif-binding factor / HY5-related light signaling factor
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout with STF4 in WT and pin3abdCR2 backgrounds; Dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; STF3pro:STF3-GFP localization/expression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss partially suppresses elevated nitrogen accumulation and cell expansion phenotypes of pin3abdCR2, suggesting reduced NUE gains if inhibited
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Light-signaling-dependent transcriptional activation of nitrate transport
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Auxin/ARF10 signaling; exogenous NAA induces STF3 expression; enhanced photosynthetic state in pin3 mutants
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes nitrate uptake by activating NPF2.13; contributes to enhanced nitrogen accumulation in pin3 higher-order mutants
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STF3 is strongly up-regulated in pin3 mutants and induced by exogenous auxin. 2. STF3 is expressed in leaf pavement cells. 3. STF3 directly binds G-box motifs in the NPF2.13 promoter and activates NPF2.13 expression. 4. Loss of STF3/4 partially suppresses the high-nitrogen phenotype of pin3abdCR2.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves nitrogen acquisition and may support enhanced photosynthesis and seed oil accumulation indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly expressed in leaf pavement cells; up-regulated ~5-fold in pin3 mutants
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NPF2.13 promoter; ARF10 upstream at STF3 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent amplifier of nitrate uptake downstream of auxin/light signaling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential partner target to enhance nitrate uptake/photosynthetic efficiency, especially with PIN3-based strategies
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf pavement cells; elevated in pin3ab and pin3abdCR2

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TGACG-motif-binding factor / HY5-related light signaling factor
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; CRISPR knockout with STF3 in pin3abdCR2 background
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss together with STF3 partially suppresses enhanced nitrogen accumulation in pin3abdCR2
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Likely transcriptional regulation, but direct targets not individually validated here
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes with STF3 to enhanced nitrate acquisition in pin3 higher-order mutants
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STF4 transcript is up-regulated in pin3 mutants. 2. STF3/4 double knockout in pin3abdCR2 background partially restores nitrogen content and pavement-cell area. 3. STF4 contributes genetically to the light-signaling module that enhances nitrate uptake.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports nitrogen acquisition and associated growth/photosynthetic traits
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated about two-fold in pin3 mutants
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supportive co-regulator in the STF module
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential modifier of NUE traits, but evidence is less direct than for STF3
